articles.title: Israeli army reports rise in sexual harassment complaints
articles.summary: Israeli army reports rise in sexual harassment complaints Israeli  army data has revealed a rise in sexual harassment complaints within the military in 2025, Israeli media  reported  on Tuesday. According to figures released by the Gender Affairs Advisor to the Chief of Staff unit, 2,420 sexual violence complaints were filed last year - around 350 more than the previous year. The unit responsible for the welfare of female soldiers said indictments were filed in only 42 cases, while 21 incidents resulted in internal disciplinary action. articles.title: Bessent Tells Allies ‘No Room for Excuses’ on Iran Sanctions
articles.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent exhorted US allies to join the US in “aggressively” enforcing sanctions on Iran and other malicious actors and said there was “no room for excuses” for those who wish to crack down on terror financing.

articles.title: ECB May 'Have to Do Something' in June, Nagel Says
articles.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council members Joachim Nagel and Francois Villeroy de Galhau discuss the energy price shock and its impact on inflation and monetary policy. "This energy supply shock is more persistent, so we are moving away from our baseline scenario," Bundesbank President Nagel says. "It means maybe we have to do something," he adds. Outgoing French central-bank chief Villeroy said he and his colleagues are “fully determined to bring inflation back to target.” They speak to Bloomberg's Oliver Crook on the sidelines of a Group of Seven meeting in Paris. (Source: Bloomberg)
